build_common_tools_justification: Standard Rust toolchain (cargo), pinned via rust-toolchain.toml.
build_floss_tools_justification: The entire toolchain (Rust, cargo, just, mdBook) is FLOSS.
build_justification: cargo build --workspace; a justfile wraps common tasks.
build_reproducible_justification: Cargo.lock pins the entire dependency graph and releases build from clean CI runners, but bit-for-bit reproducibility is not yet verified. (SUGGESTED criterion; tracked in the maturity epic #365.)
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents the required checks (fmt, clippy -D warnings, tests), conventional-commit titles, signed commits, and the RFC process: https://github.com/jensholdgaard/ourios/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
crypto_call_justification: No custom cryptography; release signing uses Sigstore/cosign infrastructure in CI, outside the product code.
crypto_certificate_verification_justific: TLS certificate verification is rustls's default and is never disabled in the codebase.
crypto_floss_justification: No cryptography implemented in the project.
crypto_keylength_justification: No cryptography implemented in the project.
crypto_password_storage_justification: The project stores no passwords or authentication credentials.
crypto_pfs_justification: No key-agreement protocols implemented in the project.
crypto_published_justification: The project implements no cryptography of its own.
crypto_random_justification: No security-sensitive random values are generated by the project (file-name UUIDs are identifiers, not security material).
crypto_tls12_justification: rustls, the TLS implementation in the dependency tree, supports only TLS 1.2 and newer.
crypto_used_network_justification: Network security uses standard protocols via vetted libraries: the object-storage client speaks HTTPS (rustls); TLS termination for OTLP ingest is delegated to the operator's infrastructure per the deployment docs. No custom protocol security.
crypto_verification_private_justificatio: Credentials (object-storage keys) are only ever sent over TLS connections whose certificates have been verified (rustls defaults).
crypto_weaknesses_justification: No cryptography implemented in the project.
crypto_working_justification: No cryptography implemented in the project.
delivery_mitm_justification: All artifacts are delivered over HTTPS from GitHub; the container image is cosign-signed and release assets carry SLSA provenance attestations (verification documented in SECURITY.md).
delivery_unsigned_justification: Signatures and provenance are verifiable per SECURITY.md; nothing is delivered over insecure channels.
description_good_justification: The README states what Ourios is (a log storage and query backend on Parquet + template mining + DataFusion) and the thesis behind it: https://github.com/jensholdgaard/ourios#readme
discussion_justification: GitHub issues and PRs, publicly searchable: https://github.com/jensholdgaard/ourios/issues
documentation_basics_justification: README plus an mdBook documentation tree (architecture, RFCs, hazards, benchmarks, glossary): https://github.com/jensholdgaard/ourios/tree/main/docs
documentation_interface_justification: The query DSL, configuration, and server roles are specified in the docs tree (docs/rfcs/, docs/architecture/); CLI surfaces are documented via --help (clap). Rustdoc quality is CI-enforced (broken intra-doc links are errors). https://github.com/jensholdgaard/ourios/tree/main/docs
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: Test and fuzz builds run with debug assertions enabled (cargo's dev/test profiles); the codebase uses debug_assert! to check producer-side invariants.
dynamic_analysis_fixed_justification: Findings become issues and fixes with committed regression cases - e.g. the RFC 0024 property suite's first run produced one same-day codec fix and one tracked schema issue (#362), with proptest counterexample files committed as permanent regressions.
dynamic_analysis_justification: A cargo-fuzz harness runs on a daily schedule (RFC 0015) and property-based suites (proptest) run in CI, including end-to-end pipeline properties over generated OTLP input (RFC 0024): https://github.com/jensholdgaard/ourios/blob/main/.github/workflows/fuzz.yml
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: The project is Rust with #![deny(unsafe_code)] enforced at every crate root; no memory-unsafe language is produced. (A fuzzer runs regardless.)
english_justification: All documentation and issue traffic is in English.
enhancement_responses_justification: Enhancement requests are triaged into epics/RFCs or dispositioned in the issue thread.
hardened_site_justification: The project site is github.com, which sets the standard security-hardening headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Content-Type-Options).
hardening_justification: The software is memory-safe Rust with #![deny(unsafe_code)] at every crate root, clippy::pedantic as errors, and bounded-resource design (RFC 0023 caps template memory; parameter byte limits bound cardinality).
homepage_url_justification: The project homepage is the GitHub repository (the mdBook docs site publishes at the first shipping milestone).
installation_common_justification: A container image (GHCR, cosign-signed) and cargo-dist release binaries with installers are published per release; a Helm chart is in progress.
maintained_justification: Actively developed; see commit and release history.
no_leaked_credentials_justification: No credentials are committed; CI secrets live in GitHub Actions secrets, local tokens in an unversioned .env (gitignored).
release_notes_justification: CHANGELOG.md plus generated notes on each GitHub release: https://github.com/jensholdgaard/ourios/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
release_notes_vulns_justification: No release so far has contained a vulnerability fix. When one does, the release notes will identify the CVE/advisory (SECURITY.md governs the process).
repo_interim_justification: main carries interim commits continuously between releases.
repo_track_justification: Git history on GitHub; main requires signed, verified commits.
report_archive_justification: GitHub issues/PRs are permanently archived and searchable.
report_process_justification: Bugs are reported via GitHub issues; CONTRIBUTING.md points there: https://github.com/jensholdgaard/ourios/issues
report_responses_justification: The maintainer triages issues actively; the issue history shows responses and dispositions on reports.
sites_https_justification: All project URLs are HTTPS on github.com.
static_analysis_common_vulnerabilities_j: GitHub CodeQL security scanning runs on every PR and OpenSSF Scorecard on a weekly schedule; results publish under Security -> Code scanning: https://github.com/jensholdgaard/ourios/security/code-scanning
static_analysis_fixed_justification: Code-scanning alerts are triaged to issues and fixed or explicitly dispositioned with documented reasons (see the triage record on issue #365 and deny.toml's justified ignore list).
static_analysis_justification: Clippy (pedantic, warnings-as-errors) and rustfmt run in CI on every PR; CodeQL code scanning and cargo-deny (advisories/licenses/bans) also gate merges: https://github.com/jensholdgaard/ourios/blob/main/.github/workflows/ci.yml
static_analysis_often_justification: Clippy and CodeQL run on every pull request and push to main.
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: GitHub Actions runs fmt, clippy, and the full test suite on every PR and push to main.
test_justification: cargo test --all-features: ~900 tests across unit, integration, property (proptest), and crash-recovery suites. CI runs them on every PR: https://github.com/jensholdgaard/ourios/blob/main/.github/workflows/ci.yml
test_most_justification: Coverage is tracked and published as a self-hosted badge in the README; correctness-critical layers (miner, storage, querier) additionally carry property-based suites and RFC acceptance gates.
test_policy_justification: Formal policy: every RFC carries section 5 acceptance scenarios that must exist as tests before the RFC is accepted; tests are treated as specifications that cannot be weakened (CLAUDE.md 6.2, CONTRIBUTING.md).
tests_are_added_justification: RFC-gated development means functionality lands with its acceptance tests; review blocks PRs that touch invariants without addressing them.
tests_documented_added_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md and CLAUDE.md 6.2 document the tests-are-specifications policy.
version_semver_justification: SemVer; breaking changes are marked with the conventional-commit ! and a BREAKING CHANGE footer, which feeds the changelog.
version_tags_justification: Git tags drive the cargo-dist release pipeline: https://github.com/jensholdgaard/ourios/tags
version_unique_justification: Releases are tagged vX.Y.Z; the workspace version is single-sourced in the root Cargo.toml.
vulnerabilities_critical_fixed_justifica: No critical-severity vulnerabilities are known in the project or unpatched in its lockfile.
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: GitHub private security advisories (Security -> Report a vulnerability), with an email fallback, per SECURITY.md.
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: SECURITY.md commits to acknowledgement within 7 days. No vulnerability reports have been received to date.
warnings_fixed_justification: CI fails on any warning; allow-lists require an in-code justification comment.
warnings_justification: rustc warnings plus clippy with -D warnings (warnings are errors) in CI.
warnings_strict_justification: clippy::pedantic is enabled workspace-wide with warnings as errors.
